name: Rina Reimer
curr-major: B.S. Applied and Computational Mathematical Sciences
abt1: I'm a software developer based in Seattle, WA. I'm currently studying math and computer science at the University of Washington. Thanks for stopping by! Be sure to check out my projects and more.
abt2: I am currently a student at the University of Washington, studying Applied and Computational Mathematical Sciences, which is a mixture of mathematics and computer science and how they relate to each other. I have challenged myself by taking difficult classes since being enrolled at UW. I have years of experience collaborating and explaining my ideas from tutoring and volunteering. I am a software developer expanding my expertise in full-stack development  through numerous personal projects. I'm always looking for opportunities to collaborate and expand my knowledge of software development.
con1: I'm looking for industry experience and other opportunities to implement programs. You can check out my personal projects or reach me by email at rinareimer616@gmail.com.

data1.title: BYJU's FutureSchool
data1.desc:
+ Successfully completed a comprehensive weekly online Python training program comprising 200+ sessions and hands-on projects, with a strong focus on machine learning and data science.
+ Developed expertise in data cleaning, data visualization, and statistical analysis through 100+ practical data science projects using large, real-world datasets.
+ Acquired practical coding experience and in-depth understanding of 10+ machine learning algorithms, including Linear/Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and Fast Fourier Transform (FFT).

data2.title: Google Data Analytics Professional Certificate
data2.desc:
+ Learned Business Intelligence concepts and technical skills, such as finding data solutions, data preparation/processing, data visualization, and how to share findings with stakeholders.
+ Harnessed learned skills through case studies and work problems, excelling in assessments throughout the program.

project1.title: Seam Carving
project1.desc: Java object-oriented program utilizing Dijkstra's graph algorithm to find the path of pixels with the least energy within an image.
project1.longdesc:
+ Java object-oriented program utilizing Dijkstra's graph algorithm to find the path of pixels with the least energy within an image.
+ 3-week long development stage, building upon CSE 373 curriculum.

project2.title: Electrical Circuit Calculator
project2.desc: Python program that calculates voltages at nodes and current along connections in a given electrical circuit.
project2.longdesc:
+ Python program that calculates voltages at nodes and current along connections in a given electrical circuit.
+ Demonstrates linear algebra expertise with Markdown explanations.

project3.title: BYJU's Capstones
project3.desc: A collection of my Python capstone projects from BYJU's FutureSchool.
project3.longdesc:
+ Excelled in weekly online Python training program, learning machine learning and data modeling techniques.
+ Mastered data cleaning/visualization and statistical representation techniques through over 100 hands-on data analysis projects with large data sets from various international sources.
+ Gained coding experience and deepened knowledge of machine learning models, such as Linear/Logistic Regression, Random Forest Classifier, and Fast Fourier Transformations.

project4.title: Bit Connect
project4.desc: Java application aimed to connect students in person using hypothetical location data and filtered matches between users based on fields of study and interests.
project4.longdesc:
+ Java application aimed to connect students in person using hypothetical location data and filtered matches between users based on fields of study and interests.
+ Teamed with fellow students using Figma, starting the project in WINFO Hackathon 2024 and continuing the implementation with help from industry professionals and self-learning.
+ 9-hour initial build with algorithm architecture draft and three-month deliverable design implementation, utilizing Gradle and the JVM to release documentation on the prototype.

project5.title: PeakMind
project5.desc: Gamified mental health app aimed at giving users accessible and friendly resources, using AI to offer personalized recommendations.
project5.longdesc:
+ Worked on a team to create a gamified mental health app aimed at giving users accessible and friendly resources, using artificial intelligence and natural language processing to offer personalized recommendations.
+ Worked on the front end to convert designs to useable interfaces, quickly picking up SwiftUI and app development.
+ Honed communication skills and time management, working on a team of 7 to deliver on a fast-paced software development cycle, earning top spots in various app design competitions.
+ Spearheaded a feature to track users’ mental health data to give users custom plans using machine learning to analyze the large data set and use various psychology sources to give accurate feedback.

project6.title: PageByPage
project6.desc: Mobile app allowing users to log and find books.
project6.longdesc:
+ Collaborated on a cross-functional team to develop an iOS app to find, log, and review books.
+ Utilized Google Cloud Platform for end-to-end development and Firebase Firestore databases to process user data, accessing book information from the Google Books API.
+ Developed app with Swift and SwiftUI, releasing the app for beta testing on the iPhone app store.
+ Completed user research to ensure high customer value and user interface.

class1.title: STAT 390: Statistical Methods in Engineering and Science
class1.desc: Exploratory data analysis and interactive computing using statistical methods in R.

class2.title: MATH 381: Discrete Mathematical Modeling
class2.desc: Introduction to methods of discrete mathematics, including topics from graph theory, network flows, and combinatorics. Emphasis on these tools to formulate models and solve problems arising in variety of applications, such as computer science, biology, and management science.

class3.title: MATH 407: Linear Optimization
class3.desc: Maximization and minimization of linear functions subject to constraints consisting of linear equations and inequalities; linear programming and mathematical modeling. Simplex method, elementary games and duality.

class4.title: AMATH 352: Applied Linear Algebra
class4.desc: LU factorization, QR factorization, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, and singular value decomposition, implemented with Python.

class5.title: AMATH 383: Introduction to Continuous Mathematical Modeling
class5.desc: Introductory survey of applied mathematics with emphasis on modeling of physical and biological problems in terms of differential equations. Formulation, solution, and interpretation of the results.

class6.title: AMATH 481: Scientific Computing
class6.desc: Survey of numerical techniques for differential equations. Emphasis is on implementation of numerical schemes for application problems.

class7.title: CSE 123: Introduction to Computer Programming III

class8.title: CSE 340: Interaction Programming
class8.desc: User interfaces for computing systems, including principles and implementation techniques. Covers key topics and programming paradigms for interactive systems, such as event handling; graphical layout, design, and widgets; undo; accessibility; and context awareness.

class9.title: CSE 373: Data Structures and Algorithms
class9.desc: Hash tables, priority queues, graphs, balanced trees, asymptotic analysis, and graph algorithms.

class10.title: CSE 374: Intermediate Programming Concepts and Tools
class10.desc: Lower-level programming and explicit memory management; use of Linux systems for compilation; software development techniques and tools including documentation and code review.

class11.title: CSE 413: Programming Languages and Implementation
class11.desc: Concepts/implementation strategies for programming languages; analysis of computer science and computer engineering performance.

class12.title: CSE 414: Introduction to Database Systems
class12.desc: Data models, query languages, transactions, database tuning, data warehousing, and parallelism.

class13.title: CSE 417: Algorithms and Computational Complexity
class13.desc: Design and analysis of algorithms and data structures. Efficient algorithms for manipulating graphs and strings. Fast Fourier Transform. Time and space complexity. NP-complete problems and undecidable problems.
